name: The Borrow Checker's Domain

enemies.name: Lifetime Error
enemies.description: Expected lifetime 'a, found lifetime 'b. They're different. Somehow.

enemies.name: Moved Value
enemies.description: Used after move. The value is gone. It was never yours.
enemies.art:
let x =
  move→
 use x?
 MOVED!

enemies.name: Unsatisfied Trait Bound
enemies.description: T does not implement Display + Clone + Send + Sync + 'static.

enemies.name: Mutable Borrow Conflict
enemies.description: Cannot borrow as mutable more than once. The rules are absolute.

enemies.name: Unsafe Block
enemies.description: unsafe { /* trust me */ } — Famous last words.

enemies.name: Generic Type Hydra
enemies.description: Where T: IntoIterator<Item = Box<dyn Fn(&mut Vec<Option<Result<T, E>>>)>>
enemies.art:
<T, U,
  V, W>
 where
  T: ??
  U: ??

bosses.name: The Borrow Checker
bosses.description: The final arbiter of ownership. Fair, strict, and absolutely merciless.

bosses.name: The Orphan Rule
bosses.description: You cannot implement a foreign trait on a foreign type. No exceptions. No mercy.

traps.name: Unwrap on None
traps.description: Called .unwrap() on a None value. Thread panicked.

traps.name: Mutex Deadlock
traps.description: Two mutexes, locked in the wrong order. Everything stops.

empty: Ownership annotations cover every surface in neat handwriting.
A cargo.lock file stretches across the floor, perfectly reproducible.
The compiler's approval stamp marks safe passages.
rest: A safe block. All borrows resolved. Rest your weary moves.
The borrow checker nods approvingly. You may rest.
A well-scoped lifetime ends peacefully here.
treasure: A perfectly derived Debug, Clone, Serialize implementation.
A crate with zero unsafe blocks. Incredibly rare.

floor_flavor: The walls enforce strict ownership semantics...
Every doorway checks your borrows before allowing passage.
Lifetime annotations grow more complex with each floor.
The air is safe. Provably, verifiably safe.
boss_clues: The ownership rules tighten with every step...
Borrow errors multiply on the walls, red and accusatory.
You feel your lifetimes being scrutinized.
